title: Procedural vs Object-Oriented Programming Paradigms
description: A side-by-side comparison showing how data and functions are organized in procedural programming versus object-oriented programming, highlighting the paradigm shift to combining both into objects.
relationships: Procedural Paradigm: Functions operate on separate data
Object-Oriented Paradigm: Objects encapsulate both data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Objects interact by sending messages (calling methods)
